name: Banana-Chocolate Bread (Hand Made)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, thoroughly mix 1 cup of flour, salt, and yeast.
2. Combine skim milk and butter or margarine in a saucepan. Heat over low heat until liquids are warm (110 to 120°F). Butter or margarine does not need to melt.
3. Gradually add the liquid mixture to dry ingredients and mix.
4. Add egg beaters, mashed bananas, chocolate pieces, and 1 cup of flour. Mix thoroughly.
5. Gradually add enough flour to make a stiff dough.
6. Turn out onto lightly floured board and knead until smooth and elastic, about 5 to 8 minutes.
7. Place in greased bowl, turning to grease the top. Cover; let rise in warm place, free from draft, until doubled in bulk, about 1 1/2 hours.
8. Punch dough down. Cover and let rise again, about 45 minutes.
9. Shape into loaf and place in greased 8-inch x 4-inch loaf pan.
10. Cover and let rise in warm place until dough fills the pan and top of loaf is about 1 inch above the top of the pan edge, about 30 minutes.
11. Heat oven to 375°F.
12. Uncover dough. Bake 30 to 35 minutes or until loaf sounds hollow when lightly tapped.
13. Immediately remove from pan; cool on wire rack.
